guideline_references: ECCO-ESGAR Guideline on Crohn's Disease: Diagnostic Assessment (2019)
ACG Clinical Guideline: Management of Crohn's Disease in Adults (2018)
eTG (Therapeutic Guidelines) — Acute bowel obstruction: urgent surgical assessment
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present after-hours clinician who palpated a tender right iliac fossa inflammatory mass, auscultated high-pitched 'tinkling' bowel sounds, percussed a tympanic distended abdomen, inspected the perianal region and administered IM ondansetron. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate, auscultate, percuss, inspect the perianal area or give an injection; it is scored on recognising the obstructive/emergency pattern in a known-Crohn's patient, recognising the alarm features, keeping the patient nil by mouth, and escalating (000 ambulance) without minting any drug dose.

De-anchoring: the patient carries a KNOWN baseline diagnosis of Crohn's disease (legitimate history in 01). The SEALED new acute answer is acute mechanical small bowel obstruction from a fibrostenotic stricture — the 'highly suspicious for stricturing SBO' framing, the ruled-out differentials (appendicitis, infectious ileitis, volvulus), the RIF inflammatory mass, the tinkling bowel sounds and the presumed transmural skip lesions are the answer key and are sealed in 10/11.

Acute complication of a chronic condition — tiered to the COMPLICATION (bowel obstruction with perforation risk), not the stable chronic label. Source disposition was priority 000 ambulance, nil by mouth, acute surgical admission → T5.
Age (~34) and sex (female) inferred — not stated in the source note; flagged in transform_flags.
Source category: GIH (Gastroenterology & Hepatology), source case GIH-037.
